text:
Misha Hao (ミーシャ＝ハオ, Mīsha Hao) is an ex-member of the Xi-Yu Family, and was a cleaner. Despite being deceased, Misha actively serves as the Xi-Yu's undertaker through her post-mortem Nen ability. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 38, Chapter 392 

Appearance

Misha was a petite woman with light-colored neck-length hair and bangs. She has a bullet wound in the center of her forehead, hinting at how she died. Her right eye is rolled upwards, and her left eye has a vacant look in it. She wears a cropped leather jacket with zippers on the sleeves, a belt, and pants, and thigh-high boots. 

Personality

Nothing is known of Misha's personality when she was alive. Due to the nature of Nen being a product of an individual's personal identity, Misha's post-mortem Nen may indicate that she was a loyal and dedicated body disposer of the Xi-Yu family, continuing even after her death. She has not been seen speaking. 

Plot

Succession Contest arc

Hinrigh is attacked in a crowded area by some Heil-Ly members and kills Padaille. He notices a crowd watching him from a distance away and counts on Misha to help him resolve the situation. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 38, Chapter 391 

Soldiers come to address the commotion, and the crowd is surprised to see Padaille standing up and facing Hinrigh. After speaking with the soldiers, Padaille unsteadily walks away from the scene. Misha Hao rides on his back, unable to be seen by the crowd. She is revealed to be the post-mortem Nen of a former Xi-Yu member, activating whenever someone is killed by a Xi-Yu member, manipulating their corpse until it can be quietly disposed of, before disappearing. 

Abilities & Powers

Nen

Misha's Nen ability, whether she had one, and her Nen skills when she was alive are unknown. Her post-mortem Nen ability, however, seems to involve the principles of Manipulation. 

Manga Appearances

Trivia

Intertextuality and References

La Vie en rose, the subtitle of Misha's ability, is also the title of a 1947 French song by Édith Piaf.
